title: What I think about IS - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking his opinion on ISIS/ISIL. He argues the problem is extreme ignorance among young people rather than Islam itself, advocates for treating refugees as part of a global community, and believes openly mocking and debating religious fundamentalism (across all religions) is the path to defusing extremism.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: It's an ignorance problem among young people, not an inherently Islamic problem
Accept and help them; a small number of bad actors are a mathematical inevitability, not a reason to reject everyone
Racist generalization is the opposite of what religion should be about
Openly drawing Muhammad is a good way to challenge and dispel fundamentalist bullying around the prohibition
Society needs to openly debate and criticize religious ideas, including abortion debates hinging on soul/life questions
Find the actual perpetrators, protect innocent Muslims from backlash, and it will resolve over time
